results.title: Scriptyard: A free visual story development workspace
results.content:
couple of years is that many writers are understandably wary of new writing tools because they’re concerned about AI and what happens to their work. Scriptyard isn’t an AI writing assistant, and it doesn’t use your writing to train AI models. It’s simply a place to develop your ideas. All files live locally, no login, no cloud. This is an independent project that I’ve been building alongside my day job, and I’d really appreciate honest feedback from writers. If something feels awkward, if something is missing, or if there’s a feature you’ve always wished these kinds of tools had, I’d love to hear it. After more than 20 years working in film production, I finally decided to build the story development tool I always wished existed. I hope some of you find it useful. Thanks! [...] Report

@roopesh\_donde Since this isn’t a project that’s monetized at all (nor ever will be) I wanted to wait and see how much of an issue this was for people. I think I might make something like a Google Drive integration so people can share access through their own space and still not need to share anything with my server. Thanks for taking the time try it and write to me!

results.title: performyard.com is rated "Great" with 4 / 5 on Trustpilot
results.content:
When I later informed them we would not be renewing, I began receiving additional invoices. That’s when I discovered the contract had been written as a 3-year term. Trying to resolve this was frustrating and disappointing. We are still paying year 3 haven't touching in 2.5 years and its not set up heavy. Our platform was 10-12k per year based on seats. Very low skill. 

I fully understand the importance of honoring contracts and transparency matters. This experience felt like a bait-and-switch, and it significantly impacted my trust.

I would not choose to work with this company again.

April 6, 2025

Image 34: Rated 5 out of 5 stars [...] The sales process felt misleading from the start. I was told multiple times on calls that the agreement was for one year, and there was urgency to sign by January 1 to receive a discounted rate. However, the contract was not actually sent until January 31, and within it, a 3-year term had been inserted without ever being discussed. I did not catch this at the time, which I take responsibility for, but it was never clearly communicated.

We paid in full for what we believed was a one-year agreement and stopped using the platform after about six months because it simply wasn’t a fit. The product felt redundant, another system to manage without adding meaningful value. Much of what it offers can be accomplished through simpler tools. [...] Apr 6, 2026

## 1 Star
1 Star – Avoid at All Costs

This company makes it nearly impossible to cancel your subscription. Despite multiple written requests to cancel, they continue to insist that I schedule a phone call—no online option, no clear path to simply stop the charges. It’s frustrating and feels like they’re just trying to trap people into paying for something they no longer want. Avoid if you value transparency and fair treatment.

## Never work for Scripted
Never work for Scripted. I spent a lot of time working on my first job for them. My pay was halved because the editor made a few basic changes and said the original work was not good enough, though the final result was almost completely mine. I complained, and they revoked my account. They never even paid me the halved amount. I'm not sure how they are able to remain in business with practices like this.

June 27, 2024

Sadly, some of the Scripted editors are woefully unqualified to hold the title of editor. I have had many issues where the editor actually created mistakes when "editing" my pieces. These aren't small mistakes either. They are glaring in-your-face mistakes which the writer ends up being blamed for since we have no way of correcting the created errors once they are sent to the client.

results.title: Script Error: What It Is and How to Fix It (With Examples)
results.content:
“Script error” has no details because the script was loaded from a different domain without the correct CORS headers. When this happens, the browser hides the filename, stack trace, and line number for security reasons.
 “Script error” does not always mean your code is broken. It usually indicates a loading or origin problem. Once the script is served with the crossorigin attribute and proper CORS headers, the actual underlying error becomes visible.
 You cannot prevent all script errors, but you can prevent the browser from hiding their details. Using crossorigin, configuring CORS on the server, and publishing source maps allow you to see what went wrong and debug it directly.

 By

 An opening voice-over plays against dissolving Texas

 VOICE-OVER  
 The world is full of complainers.  
 But the fact is, nothing comes with

 United States, or even Man of the  
 Year--something can always go wrong.  
 And go ahead, complain, tell your

 they got it mapped out so that

 that's the theory, anyway. But what

 Like maybe he's sick? Mentally?...  
 Or is it maybe me, do you think?
